title: QA: unrestricted() permission bypass has no safety-check tier; allows rm -rf / and .git writes
description:
## Severity
Critical
## Dimension
security
## Location
src/permissions.rs:113-123
## Violation
`PermissionManager::unrestricted()` constructs a manager with
`enabled = false`, which short-circuits `check()` at lines 132-134 to
`CheckResult::Allowed` for every tool call:
```rust
pub const fn unrestricted() -> Self {
    // `enabled = false` short-circuits `check()` to `CheckResult::Allowed`.
    Self {
        persisted_rules: Vec::new(),
        session_rules: Vec::new(),
        default_allow: Vec::new(),
        persist_path: PathBuf::new(),
        enabled: false,
    }
}
```
Pin tests `b3_unrestricted_allows_destructive_bash`,
`b3_unrestricted_allows_git_config_write`, and
`b3_unrestricted_allows_claude_settings_write` document that this allows
`rm -rf /`, edits to `.git/config`, and writes to `.claude/settings.json`.
There is no `safetyCheck` tier as in Claude Code's bypassPermissions mode.
The `dangerously_disable_sandbox`-from-args check at lines 155-169 is
unreachable in this mode.
## Mandated refactor
Introduce a non-bypassable `safety_check(tool, target)` invariant that
runs BEFORE the `enabled` short-circuit. At minimum reject `rm -rf /`,
writes anywhere under `.git/`, writes to `.claude/`, writes to
`/etc/openclaudia/`, and any path matching the global denylist regardless
of `enabled`. `unrestricted()` should bypass user-configured rules but
NEVER the safety-check tier.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
